fragility_points: A normal crypto downside move of more than roughly 3.6% before settlement would threaten the thesis.
Binance-specific anomalies or outages could matter more than in a generic BTC price market.
key_assumptions: Binance spot market operations remain normal through settlement.
Current BTCUSDT levels are not a transient spike likely to mean-revert below 72k by noon ET Apr 16.
Binance API data is a meaningful verification layer for the named Binance chart settlement surface.
main_logical_chain: Start from the market prior of about 90% because this is a straightforward, active crypto threshold market.
Verify the exact contract mechanics and governing source of truth on the Polymarket rules page.
Check Binance documentation and live BTCUSDT data to confirm the settlement surface family and current distance above the threshold.
Conclude that Yes is still the base case, but trim somewhat below market because single-minute and single-venue settlement risk are real.
main_thesis: The market's ~90% Yes price is broadly efficient because BTCUSDT is already around 74.7k on Binance with limited time remaining, though single-minute and single-venue settlement risk justify a slightly lower estimate.
source_quality_view: High-quality contract text plus direct Binance verification made the evidence floor adequate; independence is medium because the verification source is the same data family named by the contract.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC can move 3-4% in under two days, so the cushion is meaningful but not decisive.
The contract settles on a single Binance one-minute close, creating timing and venue fragility.
strongest_supports: Live Binance BTCUSDT checks during the run were around 74.7k, about 3.8% above the threshold.
Only about 43 hours remained until the relevant settlement minute.
Adjacent strike prices on Polymarket formed a coherent probability ladder rather than a stale isolated print.
timing_relevance: Resolution depends on the Apr 16, 2026 12:00 ET Binance BTC/USDT one-minute candle close, corresponding to 16:00 UTC during EDT.
unresolved_ambiguities: Polymarket names the Binance website chart as the settlement surface, while this run used API checks as verification rather than literal settlement output.
No dedicated volatility model was run in this pass, so the estimate remains judgment-based rather than distribution-fitted.
what_would_change_view: A drop back toward 72k before settlement, or evidence of Binance-specific operational/chart anomalies, would materially reduce confidence in Yes.
